description_long: This is a military-style helicopter with a streamlined fuselage painted in camouflage green, accented by orange panels on the nose and tail boom. The helicopter features a large main rotor with four long, black-and-white striped blades and a smaller tail rotor with four blades at the end of a slender tail boom. The cockpit is enclosed with multiple large, clear windows framed in black, providing wide visibility for the pilots. The body displays military insignia, including a white star and other markings, and the landing gear consists of two robust wheels beneath the fuselage. The overall design suggests a utility or transport role, with a sturdy build and functional features for urban or field operations.
description: A military-style green helicopter with orange accents, four main rotor blades, a four-blade tail rotor, and a cockpit with large, clear windows. It features wheeled landing gear and visible military insignia.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the helicopter, main and tail rotors, orange nose and tail, and military insignia.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the cockpit windows, nose details, and the symmetrical spread of the main rotor blades.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the tail boom, rotor configuration, and additional markings on the fuselage.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the tail rotor, tail boom, and the orange panel at the rear.
attribute_rotor_blades: Four long, straight main rotor blades, likely made of composite or metal, painted black with white stripes and yellow tips, each blade extending nearly the full width of the helicopter.
attribute_tail_rotor: Four-blade tail rotor mounted vertically at the end of the tail boom, blades are black with yellow tips, no visible protective shroud.
attribute_landing_skids: Not present; the helicopter uses a wheeled landing gear system with two main wheels visible under the fuselage.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Multiple large, rectangular cockpit windows with rounded edges, clear and slightly tinted, framed in black, providing wide forward and side visibility.
